name: openrouter
description: Access hundreds of LLMs through one OpenAI-compatible API with per-generation cost tracking via OpenRouter

tools.name: List Models
tools.description: List the models available through OpenRouter

historicalSummary.omitted: pricing, architecture, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Create Chat Completion
tools.description: Generate a chat completion from any OpenRouter model

model.description: Model ID to use, e.g. anthropic/claude-sonnet-4 or openai/gpt-4o

messages.description: Chat messages, each with role (system|user|assistant) and content

tools.name: Get Generation
tools.description: Get metadata, token counts, and cost for a past generation by ID

id.description: Generation ID returned by a chat completion (the response id field)

tools.name: Get Credits
tools.description: Get total credits purchased and used for the account (OpenRouter requires a management key for this endpoint)

prompts.title: List OpenRouter models
prompts.prompt: List the models available through OpenRouter and highlight a few with large context windows.

prompts.title: Compare model responses
prompts.prompt: Use OpenRouter to send the same prompt to two different models and compare the responses.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: OpenRouter API Setup

steps.title: Create an OpenRouter account
steps.description: Go to https://openrouter.ai and sign up or sign in. Some models offer free variants for testing; paid models require purchasing credits under Settings → Credits.

steps.title: Create an API key
steps.description: Open https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys, click 'Create Key', and optionally set a credit limit for the key.

steps.title: Store the key
steps.description: Copy the key and add it to your .env file as OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Models tool to confirm the key works, then try a chat completion with a model like openai/gpt-4o-mini.

notes: Model IDs are namespaced as vendor/model, e.g. anthropic/claude-sonnet-4
Chat completions consume prepaid credits; use the Get Generation tool to inspect the exact cost of a request
The Get Credits endpoint requires a management key per OpenRouter's docs — a regular inference key may be rejected there
